steps.title: Identify what the evidence pattern shows
steps.content: The key signal is “several other studies conducted outside North America... showed similar results to the North American studies, while few have produced results similar to those of Porvari and Verta's study.” Most studies across regions found the positive association; few found no correlation.

steps.title: Apply this pattern to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice D expresses the logical conclusion: “dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water.” When most evidence points to the positive association globally, that association is typical and widespread.

The other three contradict this—A wrongly claims the association is particular to North America when it's actually found widely, while B and C introduce irrelevant concerns the passage doesn't support.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
